zh.lifeSummary: Margaret Beck於1468年出生在英國波克夏提勒爾斯特。她於1491年與William Bushnell在英國波克夏結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1563年8月在她的家鄉享年95歲，埋葬在英國波克夏提勒爾斯特。

nameMeanings.name: Beck
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived beside a stream, from northern Middle English bekke ‘stream’ (Old Norse bekkr).

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of various places in northern France named Bec, for example Bec Hellouin in Eure, named with Old Norman French bec ‘stream’, from the same Old Norse root as in 1 above.
English: from the Middle English personal name Becke (Old English Becca or Beocca), of uncertain origin.

variants.name: Beckett
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): nickname from Old French, Middle English beket ‘small beak’ (a diminutive of Old French bec ‘beak or mouth’), perhaps with reference to someone with a small beak-like mouth or a prominent nose (compare Beck 4), though the Middle English word appears to have had a wide range of applications denoting ‘pointed objects’, including ‘corbel, kind of arrow, kind of bird’, and ‘kind of fish’. This surname is also found in Ireland, especially in Antrim.

English: habitational name from any of the places called Beckett in Berkshire and Devon. The former is named with Old English bēo ‘bee’ + cot ‘cottage, shelter’; the latter has as its first element the Old English personal name Bicca.
Altered form of   Bequette  , a surname of French origin.

variants.name: Beckford
meanings: English: habitational name from a place now in Worcestershire (formerly in Gloucestershire) named Beckford, from the Middle English name Becke (see Beck 3) + Old English ford ‘ford’. This surname is most frequent in Jamaica.
History: The surname Beckford was taken to Jamaica in the 17th century. Colonel Peter Beckford (1643–1710) served as acting governor of Jamaica in 1702.

variants.name: Beckemeyer
meanings: North German: distinguishing name for a tenant farmer living by a stream (see   Beck   +   Meyer  ).

variants.name: Beckham
meanings: English (Norfolk): habitational name from a place in Norfolk named Beckham, from the Middle English name Becke (see Beck 3) + Old English hām ‘homestead’.

variants.name: Becker
meanings: German and Dutch: occupational name for a baker of bread, or brick and tiles, derived from Middle High German and Middle Dutch backen ‘to bake’. The German surname is also found in France (mainly Lorraine and Alsace).
Danish: occupational name for a baker, from bechere ‘baker’.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name for a baker, from German Bäcker (compare 1 and Backer ) or Yiddish beker ‘baker’.

variants.name: Beckius
meanings: Swedish: ornamental name composed of the element beck, a variant of bäck ‘stream’, + the Latin suffix -ius.
German: Latinized (humanistic) form of Beck ‘baker’ or ‘stream’. The surname Beckius is very rare in Germany.

variants.name: Becka
meanings: Czech and Slovak (Bečka): nickname or metonymic occupational name from bečka ‘cask’.
German: variant of   Beck   ‘baker’.

variants.name: Becken
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Thor.
North German: variant of   Beck   6.
Norwegian: very rare variant, and an Americanized form, of   Bekken  .

variants.name: Becking
meanings: Dutch and German: farm name, mainly in the border region of East Netherlands (Overijssel) and Germany (North Rhine-Westphalia), indicating its location ‘beside a stream’ (see   Beck   6).

variants.name: Beckley
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the places so named in Oxfordshire and Sussex, from the Old English byname Becca (see Beck 3) + Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’.
English: variant of   Bickley  .
Americanized form of South German Bächle (see Baechle ) and of its Swiss German cognate Bächli.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Beck was born in 1468, in Tilehurst, Berkshire, England. She married William Bushnell in 1491, in Berkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 5 daughters. She died in August 1563, in her hometown, at the age of 95, and was buried in Tilehurst, Berkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret Beck est née en 1468, à Tilehurst, Berkshire, Angleterre. Elle a épousé William Bushnell en 1491, à Berkshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée en août 1563, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 95 ans, et a été enterrée à Tilehurst, Berkshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret Beck wurde 1468 in Tilehurst, Berkshire, England geboren. Sie hat 1491 in Berkshire, England William Bushnell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb im August 1563 im Alter von 95 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Tilehurst, Berkshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret Beck è nata il nel 1468 nel Tilehurst, Berkshire, England. Lei sposato William Bushnell nel 1491, nel Berkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 5 figlie. Morì nel agosto 1563, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 95 anni, e fu sepolta a Tilehurst, Berkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Beckは1468年にイギリスバークシュアイアティレハルストで生まれた。1491年にイギリスバークシュアイアでWilliam Bushnellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1563年8月に故郷で95歳で死去し，イギリスバークシュアイアティレハルストにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Beck(은)는 1468년 영국 버크실르 틸후르스트에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1491년 영국 버크실르에서 William Bushnell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1563년 8월 그녀의 고향에서 95세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 버크실르 틸후르스트에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Margaret Beck nasceu em 1468, em Tilehurst, Berkshire, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com William Bushnell em 1491, em Berkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em agosto de 1563, em sua cidade natal, com 95 anos, e foi sepultada em Tilehurst, Berkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret Beck родилась в 1468 г. в местности тилэурст, биркшир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Bushnell в 1491 г. в местности биркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла август 1563 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 95 лет и была похоронена в местности тилэурст, биркшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Margaret Beck nació en 1468, en Tilehurst, Berkshire, Inglaterra. Se casó con William Bushnell en 1491, en Berkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió en agosto de 1563, en su pueblo, a la edad de 95 años, y fue sepultada en Tilehurst, Berkshire, Inglaterra.
